segment.name: ServiceStringAdvice
segment.desc: To define the characters selected for use as delimiters and indicators in the rest of the interchange that follows

segment.name: InterchangeHeader
segment.desc: To start, identify and specify an interchange

data_element.desc: Unique reference assigned by sender
Optionally message identification if the interchange contains only one type of message
Used if specified in IA
Set = 1 if sender requests acknowledgement, i.e. UNB and UNZ segments received and identified
If used, to identify type of communication agreement controlling the interchange, e.g. Customs or ECE agreement. Code or name as specified in IA
Set = 1 if the interchange is a test. Otherwise not used

segment.name: InterchangeTrailer
segment.desc: To end and check the completeness of an interchange

segment.name: FunctionalGroupHeader
segment.desc: To head, identify and specify a Functional Group

data_element.desc: Identifies the one message type in the functional group
Unique reference number assigned by sender's division, department etc.
Code to identify the agency controlling the specification, maintenance and publication of the message type
Password to recipient's division, department or sectional system (if required)

segment.name: FunctionalGroupTrailer
segment.desc: To end and check the completeness of a Functional Group

data_element.desc: The count of the number of messages in the functional group
Shall be identical to 0048 in UNG

segment.name: MessageHeader
segment.desc: To head, identify and specify a Message

data_element.desc: to relate all subsequent transfers of data to the same business case or file. Within the 35 characters the IA may specify component elements

segment.name: MessageTrailer
segment.desc: To end and check the completeness of a Message

data_element.desc: Control count including UNH and UNT
Shall be identical to 0062 in UNH

segment.name: Text
segment.desc: To give information in addition to that in other segments in the service message, as required

data_element.desc: Qualifies and identifies the purpose and function of the segment if indicated in the message specification
Not machine-processable information

segment.name: SectionControl
segment.desc: To separate Header, Detail and Summary sections of a message

data_element.desc: Separates sections in a message by one of the following codes: D separates the header and detail sections; S separates the detail and summary sections

segment.name: MessageTrailer
segment.desc: To end and check the completeness of a Message

data_element.desc: Control count including UNH and UNT
Shall be identical to 0062 in UNH
